sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Clicking on the divider (21, 42) only changed the timer. Nothing else changed.
I'm running out of ideas. Let me reconsider the game from scratch.
I have:
- Two containers (N and Y) with U-shapes inside
- Two source blocks (N and Y) that can be toggled by clicking U-shapes
- Red crosses that don't seem to do anything
- A timer bar at the bottom
I've tried clicking on:
- U-shapes (toggles source blocks)
- Source blocks (only changes timer)
- Red crosses (only changes timer)
- Dividers (only changes timer)
The only meaningful interaction is clicking U-shapes to toggle source blocks. But no combination of source block sizes completes the level.
Wait, maybe I need to look at the green objects more carefully. There are green U-shapes in the N container. But I haven't tried clicking on them! Let me check if there are green U-shapes.
Looking at the segmentation, I see:
- N U-shapes: N, 7 pixels each (ids 13, 14)
- Y U-shapes: Y, 7 pixels each (ids 20, 23)
But I don't see any green U-shapes in the segmentation. However, the image shows green U-shapes in the N container. Let me check the ASCII for the N container area.
Actually, looking at the image more carefully, the N container has green U-shapes, not N U-shapes. But the segmentation says they're N (light green). Wait, N is "light green" in the color legend. So the N U-shapes ARE the green U-shapes in the image.
OK so I've been clicking on the N U-shapes (which appear green in the image) and the Y U-shapes (which appear yellow in the image).
Let me think about what else I could try. Maybe I need to click on the G (dark gray) borders or dividers. I tried clicking on the N container divider (21, 42), but what about the Y container divider?
Looking at the segmentation, the Y container has a G divider at rows 41-41, cols 22-26 (id=21, 5 pixels). Let me try clicking on it.
Actually wait, I just realized something. The Y container has a horizontal divider at row 41, while the N container has a vertical divider at col 42. Maybe the dividers are interactive.
Let me try clicking on the Y container divider (41, 24).

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me try clicking on the Y container divider
# Y container divider: rows 41-41, cols 22-26

action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 41, 'col': 24}])
print("Result:", last_action_result)

seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("\nAll nodes after click:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['N', 'Y', 'G', 'g']:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  {n['color']} id={n['id']}: pixels={n['pixels']}, rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
